WildLevelMin.Description: Level for this Pkmn to spawn with in the wild

WildLevelMax.Description: Level for this Pkmn to spawn with in the wild

SpawnAttack.Description: Attack interaction used to initialise Pokémon stats on spawn.

Type1.Description: Primary elemental type of this Pokémon (e.g. Fire, Water, Grass, Rock, Ground, Electric, Ice, Poison, Psychic, Bug, Ghost, Dragon, Flying, Fighting, Normal). Used to compute damage resistances and weaknesses.

Type2.Description: Secondary elemental type (optional). Leave empty for single-type Pokémon.

CanEvolve.Description: Whether this NPC can be evolved.

EvolveRequiredLevel.Description: The level required to evolve this NPC.

EvolveRoleChange.Description: The role the NPC will change into when it's evolved.

EvolveRequiredItem.Description: The list of items that are deemed attractive (there's a chance targets holding them will be followed).

IsMountable.Description: Whether this NPC can be mounted. This only triggers a reaction, as mounting requires the NPC to be tamed.

IsTameable.Description: Whether this NPC can be tamed.

TameRoleChange.Description: The role the NPC will change into when it's tamed.

TameRequiredAttitudes.Description: The attitude groups of players that can tame the NPC.

ParticleOffset.Description: The offset of any spawned particle systems.

WanderRadius.Description: The wander radius of the NPC.

ViewRange.Description: The view distance of the NPC, in blocks.

ViewSector.Description: The view sector of the NPC, in degrees.

HearingRange.Description: The hearing distance of the NPC, in blocks.

AbsoluteDetectionRange.Description: The range at which a target is guaranteed to be detected, in blocks. If set to zero, absolute detection will be disabled. The interval [ AbsoluteDetectionRange, AlertedActionRange ] defines the distance range within which an NPC can be befriended.

FleeRange.Description: How far to try and get away when fleeing.

ChanceToBeAlertedWhenFlockAlerted.Description: Percentage chance to engage in combat if another flock member engages a target.

AlertedRange.Description: The range within which the target can be seen/sensed when the NPC is alerted to their presence, in blocks. Should be higher than sense ranges.

AlertedTime.Description: How long to wait in an alerted or threatening state before switching to active combat.

MaintainDistanceWhileAlerted.Description: Whether or not to maintain distance and strafe while in the alerted state.

AlertedRelativeForwardsSpeed.Description: Relative movement speed when moving forwards or strafing while in the alerted state. Only applies if MaintainDistanceWhileAlerted is true.

AlertedStrafingDurationRange.Description: How long to strafe for when in the alerted state. Only applies if MaintainDistanceWhileAlerted is true.

AlertedStrafingFrequencyRange.Description: How frequently to strafe when in the alerted state. Only applies if MaintainDistanceWhileAlerted is true.

FleeIfNotThreatened.Description: Whether to flee while in the alerted state if not threatened after AlertedTime expires.

ThreatenAnimation.Description: An optional animation to play to threaten the target before initiating combat.

ThreatenDelayRange.Description: Delay before the threaten animation is played.

AlertedActionRange.Description: The range at which an NPC will react to other NPCs and players, depending on the attitude towards them, in blocks. Should be higher than AbsoluteDetectionRange to make the NPC befriendable.

LeashDistance.Description: The range at which an NPC will start to want to return to their spawn point, in blocks.

LeashMinPlayerDistance.Description: The minimum distance from the player before the NPC will be willing to give up on the chase.

LeashTimer.Description: How long the NPC must be more than the minimum distance form the player and too far from leash before giving up.

HardLeashDistance.Description: An absolute maximum from the the leash position the NPC can go before turning back.

LeashRelativeSpeed.Description: The relative speed at which the NPC will return to its leash position

ConfusedTimeRange.Description: How long the NPC should act confused for when losing its target before beginning the search. While confused the NPC detection range is lowered.

SearchTimeRange.Description: How long the NPC should actively search for its lost target before giving up and going back to idle.

AttractiveItemSet.Description: The list of items that are deemed attractive (there's a chance targets holding them will be followed).

AttractiveItemSetParticles.Description: The particle system that spawns to indicate the NPC's liked item(s).

WeightFollowItem.Description: The probability the NPC will follow an attractive item when held by a non-hostile target, in percent.

FollowItemAlways.Description: Whether the NPC will follow an attractive item indefinitely. If set to false, make sure FollowItemTimeout is properly configured.

FollowItemPreDelay.Description: The time delay before an NPC starts following an item.

FollowItemTimeout.Description: The duration of the follow item behaviour, after which the NPC will give up on the target. Requires FollowItemAlways to be set to false.

FlockArray.Description: The set of NPCs belonging to this flock type

ClimbHeight.Description: The maximum climb height, in blocks.

AscentAnimationType.Description: Animation when climbing up. Options: Walk, Jump, Climb, Fly, Idle.

DescendSpeedCompensation.Description: Factor to compensate forward speed reduction while moving downwards.

DescendFlatness.Description: Relative scale how fast NPC moves forward while climbing down.

DescentSteepness.Description: The relative steepness of the descent.

DescentBlending.Description: Blending of the descent pattern. 0 is linear, higher values more curved.

PlayerDefaultAttitude.Description: The default attitude towards the player.

AlertedTimeoutGiveUp.Description: The timeout after which an alerted NPC will give up on the target, in seconds.

GrazingBlockSet.Description: The type of block that this NPC will graze on top of.

FoodBlockSet.Description: The type of block that this NPC will occasionally seek out to munch on.

DayTimePeriod.Description: The hour range considered to be daytime for this NPC (inclusive). Hours should be written with decimal fractions.

DayTimeNapWeight.Description: The weight used to determine if the NPC should have a short nap during the day. Set to 0 to disable.

DayTimeNapTimeRange.Description: The duration range of daytime naps, in seconds.

TimidDuringDay.Description: Whether this NPC is a bit more timid during the day.

AlwaysSleepAtNight.Description: Whether this NPC always sleeps through the whole night.

DayFlavorAnimation.Description: The flavor animation to play during the day. Ignored if null or empty.

NightFlavorAnimation.Description: A flavor animation to play at night. Ignored if null or empty.

NightFlavorAnimationLength.Description: How long to play the night flavor animation.

IgnoreOverrideTime.Description: The duration of the ignore attitude override for the target.

ProduceItem.Description: The item this NPC produces (poop, eggs, etc).

DayFlavorAnimationLength.Description: How long the daytime flavor animation will be played for, in seconds.

Timid.Description: Whether this NPC is timid or not. Timid NPCs are harder to approach and befriend.

UseCombatActionEvaluator.Description: Whether to use the Combat Action Evaluator via an accompanying CombatConfig. This completely replaces all other combat behavior but still uses movement properties such as strafe durations and move speeds.

AttackWhenStartled.Description: Whether this NPC attacks when startled.

Attack.Description: The attack interaction this NPC will use.

AttackRange.Description: The range at which the NPC can identify an attacker.

MaxSpeed.Description: The maximum walking speed of the NPC.

MaxHealth.Description: The maximum health of the NPC.

MaxRotationSpeed.Description: The maximum rotation speed, in degrees per second.

RunThreshold.Description: The speed ratio at which the NPC switches to run animation (e.g if MaxSpeed is 10, and RunThreshold is set to 0.55, the NPC will start running at speed 5.5).

Chase.Description: Whether the NPC will chase the target during combat.

ChaseRelativeSpeed.Description: Relative chasing speed when pursuing a target.

CombatMovingRelativeSpeed.Description: Relative speed to use during combat specific movement (e.g. to achieve slower strafing).

AttackPauseRange.Description: The range for absolute minimum time before an NPC can execute a second attack.

CombatRelativeTurnSpeed.Description: Modifier that decides turn speed difference in combat.

CombatBehaviorDistance.Description: The distance within which to switch from smart chasing to combat-oriented movement behavior.

CombatAlwaysMovingWeight.Description: Weight for the combat movement behavior where the npcs will always chharge at you,put 0 to disable.

CombatDirectWeight.Description: Weight for the direct combat movement behavior (i.e. just going straight at the target, maintaining a close distance and attacking. Set to 0 to disable.

CombatStrafeWeight.Description: Weight for the combat strafing movement behavior. Set to 0 to disable.

CombatAttackPreDelay.Description: The time delay range before the NPC performs an attack, provided it's in attack range.

CombatAttackPostDelay.Description: The time delay range after the NPC performs an attack, provided it's in attack range.

CombatStrafingDurationRange.Description: How long to strafe for when in the combat strafing movement behavior. Only applies if CombatStrafeWeight is greater than 0.

CombatStrafingFrequencyRange.Description: How frequently to strafe when in the combat strafing movement behavior. Only applies if CombatStrafeWeight is greater than 0.

CombatBackOffAfterAttack.Description: Whether to back off after performing an attack.

CombatBackOffDistanceRange.Description: How far to back off after performing an attack. Max should be lower than the CombatBehaviorDistance.

CombatBackOffDurationRange.Description: How long to back off for after performing an attack.

TargetSwitchTimer.Description: Minimum time the NPC will be locked onto a single target before switching

TargetRange.Description: The distance the target needs to be within to maintain priority over other targets while in combat

BlockAbility.Description: The interaction to use for blocking when backing away. Set to empty to disable blocking.

BlockProbability.Description: The probability this NPC will block when backing away if a BlockAbility is set.

CombatFleeIfTooCloseDistance.Description: In combat, if the target gets within this distance for a certain amount of time, the NPC will attempt to flee to a safe distance instead of simply backing away. Set to 0 to disable behavior.

AdditionalCombatBehaviorMacroElement.Description: An optional macro element for supplying more bespoke combat movement behavior. Weight for the behavior must be internally defined in the macro element.

BlockStepDetectionBlockSet.Description: Optional blockset that this NPC will passively detect steps on.

BlockStepDetectionRange.Description: Detection range for the block step detection.

BlockStepDetectionIncludePlayers.Description: Whether block steps from players will be detected.

BlockStepDetectionIncludeNPCs.Description: Whether block steps from NPCs will be detected.

BlockStepDetectionRelativeSpeed.Description: The relative motion speed at which the NPC will investigate a stepped block

BlockStepDetectionIgnoreGroups.Description: The NPC groups whose steps will be ignored.

IsMemory.Description: Whether the NPC is a memory.

MemoriesNameOverride.Description: If set, overrides the memory name.

BreathesInWater.Description: Whether this NPC can breathe underwater.

NameTranslationKey.Description: The translation key for the NPC name display

PokemonType1.Description: Primary elemental type of this Pokémon (e.g. Fire, Water, Grass, Rock, Ground, Electric, Ice, Poison, Psychic, Bug, Ghost, Dragon, Flying, Fighting, Normal). Used to compute damage resistances and weaknesses.

PokemonType2.Description: Secondary elemental type (optional). Leave empty for single-type Pokémon.

StateTransitions.$Comment: Always clear animation when moving from Alerted or Flee state.
Always clear target and reset instructions when going back to idle.

Interactions.$Comment: Config for a successful melee hit, default values for Effects and HitShape are not overwritten here, see each NPC_ file

Instructions.$Comment: Init — runs once on spawn to set Pokémon stats, then transitions to Idle
Join a flock. This is separate so it can run all the time regardless of the rest of the script
Update flock member leash positions to match the leader.
Join eligible flocks if not a member of a flock.
Switch to combat state if any flock member engages a target.
If there's someone who could have caused the damage, target them.
If the damage was from an unknown source, panic to try and get away.
Check if there are any potential threats nearby

Filters.$Comment: Necessary for passable blocks such as webs

Instructions.$Comment: If we're beyond our soft leash radius for a set period of time, return home.
Execute nighttime idle behaviors if we're an NPC that doesn't always sleep at night.
Very low chance of sleeping at night since they'll stay asleep all night then.
Wake up if an enemy attacks a flock member.
Used when receiving something like a flock beacon that may potentially result in the NPC becoming alerted but could also be discarded with a random chance.
If the target moves, start chase
TODO: This Continue causes moving while attacking to maintain distance

Actions.$Comment: TODO: Brief delay before attack so NPC can stop moving. Do we need it?
Brief delay to prevent the NPC potentially strafing/backing away and missing the shot.

Instructions.$Comment: Strafe around the target while fighting. - this strafes to a very short distance, changing it to maintain > attack distance will lead to the NPC never attacking

Instructions.$TODO: Maybe we can transfer some of this logic over to the Generic Search macro element instead

Instructions.$Comment: Investigate the location where a BlockSet was disturbed
Act confused for a short period where detection range is drastically lowered so the target has a chance to get away properly
Search the area for a bit with the expectation of going straight into combat if you find something.
If the target gets too close and the NPCs isn't shy, attack it
Start a timer to switch to combat if it can't flee from the target
Go back to idle if target is lost for some reason
Get out of the water! This is separate so it can run all the time regardless of the rest of the script, when in water go straight to panic to get out
Initialize the produce alarm. This prevents NPCs from producing as soon as they spawn in
Clear the produce alarm when it finishes

Sensor.$Comment: Check the negative first so we can exit early and avoid the rest of the logic
Check the negative first so we can exit early and avoid the rest of the logic

Instructions.$Comment: Only now that we've failed to match all possible interactions do we set interactability to false.
